title: 10 best herbal capsules for bad breath
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for bad breath are dietary supplements that contain a blend of natural ingredients, such as chlorophyll, parsley, coriander, and cinnamon, which are known for their antibacterial and deodorizing properties. These capsules work by neutralizing the sulfur compounds that cause bad breath, promoting fresh and clean breath. An example of herbal capsules for bad breath is Nature's Way Chlorophyll, which contains chlorophyllin, a water-soluble form of chlorophyll. By taking these capsules, individuals can improve their oral hygiene, boost their confidence, and enjoy social interactions without the worry of bad breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsule helps with bad breath because it contains antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Its active compounds, such as citronellal and geraniol, have been shown to inhibit the growth of oral bacteria. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. Furthermore, lemon balm has a refreshing and calming effect on the digestive system, which can help prevent bad breath caused by indigestion or acid reflux. Overall, lemon balm capsules can be an effective and natural way to combat bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Lemon balm has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria and microorganisms responsible for bad breath, with rosmarinic acid and citronellal being the main active constituents.
Carminative: Its carminative properties help prevent the formation of intestinal gas and bloating, which can cause bad breath, due to the presence of volatile oils like citral and geraniol.

Anxiolytic: Lemon balm has anxiolytic effects that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can lead to bad breath by increasing mouth breathing and saliva drying, thanks to its rosmarinic acid content.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of lemon balm help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to bad breath, with rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and ferulic acid as the primary antioxidant compounds.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can exacerbate bad breath, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, with eugenol and ursolic acid being the main anti-inflammatory constituents.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in medicinal tea for bad breath due to their high concentration of antioxidants and essential oils, which have antibacterial properties that can help reduce bad breath.

Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them useful for treating bad breath caused by bacterial infections.

Stems: Although often overlooked, the stems of lemon balm contain rosmarinic acid, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, and can help reduce bad breath by neutralizing the volatile sulfur compounds that cause it.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves, crushing them gently to release their oils.
Combine the crushed lemon balm leaves with 1 teaspoon of baking soda in a small bowl.
Add just enough water to form a thick paste, mixing well to ensure all ingredients are combined.
Fill size 00 gelatin capsules with the lemon balm-baking soda paste, using a capsule-filling tool.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24 hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsule helps with bad breath because it contains essential oils with antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath. The main active component, eucalyptol, has been shown to have potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help eliminate the causes of halitosis. Additionally, the capsules can help promote saliva production, which is essential for washing away food particles and bacteria that can lead to bad breath. By reducing the bacterial load and maintaining a healthy oral environment, Tasmanian blue gum capsules can help freshen breath and improve overall oral hygiene.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Tasmanian blue gum capsules can help reduce bad breath by inhibiting the growth of microorganisms responsible for causing oral odor. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpinen-4-ol, α-terpineol, and 1,8-cineole.

Anti-inflammatory: This property helps reduce inflammation in the oral cavity, which can contribute to bad breath. The active constituents responsible for this property include α-pinene, β-pinene, and limonene.

Antioxidant: Tasmanian blue gum capsules can help neutralize free radicals in the mouth, reducing oxidative stress and associated bad breath. The active constituents responsible for this property include γ-terpinene, rutin, and quercetin.

Wound healing: This property promotes the healing of oral wounds and sores, which can help reduce bad breath caused by infection or inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include eucalyptol, α-terpineol, and α-pinene.

Immune support: Tasmanian blue gum capsules can help boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that can cause bad breath. The active constituents responsible for this property include 1,8-cineole, α-pinene, and β-pinene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum capsule plant have antibacterial properties that can help eliminate the bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath.
Bark: The bark of the plant contains tannins that have antiseptic properties, which can help kill off the germs causing bad breath.
Flowers: The flowers of the Tasmanian blue gum capsule plant contain essential oils that have a refreshing scent, which can help mask bad breath.
Seeds: The seeds of the plant contain oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, thereby preventing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Tasmanian blue gum leaves and 0.5-1 teaspoon of baking soda.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground leaves and baking soda in a small bowl.
Fill size 000 empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture using a capsule filling machine.
Store the completed capsules in a tightly sealed container for up to 6 months. Take 1-2 capsules daily as needed to freshen breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsule helps with bad breath because it contains thymol, a natural compound with powerful antiseptic and antibacterial properties. These properties make it effective in killing the bacteria that cause bad breath, particularly those that live on the tongue and in the mouth. Additionally, thyme has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, contributing to fresher breath. Furthermore, thyme has a pleasant aroma that can help mask any lingering bad odors, making it a natural and effective solution for bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Thyme capsules contain thymol and carvacrol, which are effective in killing the bacteria responsible for causing bad breath.
Antifungal properties: The antifungal properties of thyme help in preventing the overgrowth of fungi and yeast in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help alleviate bad breath caused by inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme is rich in antioxidants, which help neutralize free radicals in the body and protect against cell damage, including damage to the cells in the mouth that can cause bad breath.

Expectorant properties: Thyme has expectorant properties that help loosen and expel mucus from the respiratory tract, which can help reduce bad breath caused by postnasal drip or respiratory infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in antimicrobial compounds that help to eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain essential oils with antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing bad breath.
Stems: Thyme stems contain tannins that have astringent properties, helping to control bad breath by reducing mouth bacteria.
Seeds: Thyme seeds are a rich source of thymol, a potent antimicrobial agent that helps to eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves, rinsing them gently and allowing to air dry for 24 hours.
Grind dried thyme leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 500mg of thyme powder per capsule, filling size "0" vegetarian capsules using a capsule machine.
Seal each capsule by compressing the two halves together, storing in an airtight container.
Take one capsule twice daily, preferably after meals, for managing bad breath.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsule helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and astringent properties that help to reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. Sage has been used traditionally for its medicinal benefits, including its ability to freshen breath. The herb works by neutralizing the bacteria that cause bad breath, leaving the mouth feeling clean and fresh. Additionally, sage has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe inflamed gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. By taking sage capsules, you can help to maintain a healthy oral environment and keep bad breath at bay.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Sage capsules can help with bad breath by reducing the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath, due to the presence of active compounds such as camphor, cineole, and thujone.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help alleviate bad breath caused by inflammation, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and other phenolic acids.
Astringent properties: Sage has a drying effect on the mouth, which can help reduce bad breath caused by excess saliva and bacteria, due to the presence of tannins.

Antioxidant properties: Sage can help neutralize free radicals in the mouth and reduce oxidative stress, which can help prevent bad breath caused by cell damage, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antifungal properties: Sage can help inhibit the growth of fungi in the mouth, such as Candida albicans, which can cause bad breath, due to the presence of active compounds such as thujone and camphor.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the sage capsule plant have antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate the bacteria that cause bad breath.
Stems: The stems of the sage capsule plant contain tannins and essential oils that have a drying effect in the mouth, helping to reduce bad breath.
Flowers: The flowers of the sage capsule plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe irritation in the mouth and throat, reducing bad breath.
Seeds: The seeds of the sage capsule plant contain thujone, a compound that has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective in reducing bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather and finely chop 2 tablespoons of fresh sage leaves, allowing them to dry for 24 hours.
Mix the dried sage with 1 cup of empty vegetarian capsule powder, following the manufacturer's instructions for filling the capsules.
Fill 100-150 size 00 vegetarian capsules with the sage-powder mixture, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the filled sage capsules in an airtight container, in a cool, dry place, for up to 6 months.
Take 1-2 capsules daily, 30 minutes before meals, to reduce bad breath over time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano capsule helps with bad breath because it contains antimicrobial compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Additionally, oregano has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. Furthermore, oregano oil has been shown to have potent antioxidant properties, which can help protect the mouth and throat from damage caused by free radicals, thereby promoting overall oral health and fresher breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Oregano capsules can help reduce bad breath by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that cause bad breath, due to the presence of carvacrol and thymol.
Anti-inflammatory: Oregano capsules can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can help reduce bad breath caused by inflammation, due to the presence of beta-caryophyllene.
Antioxidant: Oregano capsules can help neutralize harmful free radicals in the body, which can help reduce bad breath caused by oxidative stress, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid.
Immune-boosting: Oregano capsules can help strengthen the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that can cause bad breath, due to the presence of terpinene and limonene.

Digestive aid: Oregano capsules can help improve digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help reduce bad breath caused by poor digestion and gut health, due to the presence of thymol and carvacrol.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Often used in medicinal teas for bad breath due to their antibacterial properties that can help reduce oral bacteria.
Flowers: Contain antioxidants and essential oils that can freshen breath and promote oral health.
Seeds: Rich in thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate the cause of bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried oregano leaves and 1 cup of pure water; steep oregano in boiling water for 10 minutes to create a strong oregano tea.
Strain the tea to remove oregano leaves, allowing tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Measure 1 teaspoon of powdered vegetarian capsule filler and mix it evenly with the cooled oregano tea.
Fill size 00 vegetarian capsules with the oregano tea and filler mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry completely before storing them in a cool, dark place, and take two capsules daily for bad breath relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsule helps with bad breath because it contains antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that fight against the bacteria and microorganisms causing bad breath. Fennel is also known for its breath-freshening qualities, as it contains essential oils that stimulate saliva production, helping to wash away odor-causing particles in the mouth. Additionally, fennel has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to bad breath. By taking fennel capsules, you can help eliminate the source of bad breath and maintain fresh breath throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Fennel capsules can inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi responsible for bad breath, thanks to the presence of active constituents like trans-anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Carminative: Fennel helps prevent the formation of intestinal gas and reduces bloating, which can indirectly contribute to bad breath caused by digestive issues. This property is attributed to the presence of volatile oils, including estragole and fenchone.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, potentially alleviating bad breath caused by irritation or infection. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.

Antioxidant: Fennel contains antioxidants that help protect cells from damage and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to bad breath. Key antioxidants in fennel include rosmarinic acid, quercetin, and various flavonoids.

Breath-freshening: Fennel has a natural, refreshing aroma that helps mask unpleasant odors in the mouth, providing immediate relief from bad breath. This property is attributed to the presence of volatile oils, particularly trans-anethole and estragole.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in oil that can help reduce bad breath by killing the bacteria in the mouth.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have antimicrobial properties that can help freshen breath.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain compounds that can neutralize foul odors, making them effective for treating bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of fennel seeds and grind them into a fine powder, which should take approximately 2-3 minutes.
Mix the ground fennel seeds with 1 teaspoon of pure baking soda, ensuring they are well combined.
Add just enough water to create a thick paste, mixing for about 1 minute.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin capsules with the fennel-baking soda paste, making sure to evenly distribute the mixture between 12 capsules.
Allow the filled capsules to dry at room temperature for 24 hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bog myrtle capsule helps with bad breath because it contains powerful natural compounds that neutralize odor-causing bacteria in the mouth. The herb's essential oils, such as eucalyptol and limonene, have antimicrobial properties that eliminate the bacteria responsible for bad breath. Furthermore, bog myrtle has anti-inflammatory effects that reduce inflammation in the gums, promoting better oral health and fresher breath. Regular consumption of bog myrtle capsules can help maintain a healthy oral environment, leading to long-lasting fresh breath.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Bog myrtle contains compounds such as alphitolic acid and betulonic acid that help to inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for causing bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory: The plant's essential oils, including myrcene and limonene, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath.

Antioxidant: Bog myrtle is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and tannins, that can help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can improve overall oral health and reduce bad breath.
Astringent: The tannins in bog myrtle have astringent properties that can help to tighten and tone gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum disease and bad breath.

Anesthetic: Bog myrtle contains numbing agents such as myrtenal and myrtenol, which can help to relieve pain and discomfort in the mouth and throat, making it easier to maintain good oral hygiene and prevent bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the bog myrtle plant are rich in antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making them effective in reducing bad breath caused by oral bacteria.
Flowers: The flowers of the bog myrtle plant have a pleasant aroma and contain tannins that can help eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath.
Buds: The buds of the bog myrtle plant contain high levels of essential oils that have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating bad breath.
Stems: The stems of the bog myrtle plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help eliminate the bacteria causing bad breath.
Bark: The bark of the bog myrtle plant contains tannins and essential oils that have antiseptic and astringent properties, making it effective in treating bad breath.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried bog myrtle leaves and stems, ensuring they are organically grown and free of pesticides.
Grind the bog myrtle leaves and stems into a fine powder using a clean coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, which should take approximately 5 minutes.
Measure out 500mg of the ground bog myrtle powder per capsule using a precision scale, filling empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the powder.
Seal the capsules using a capsule machine, ensuring a tight fit to preserve the herb's potency.
Store the finished bog myrtle capsules in a cool, dry, and dark place, such as an airtight container, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cilantro capsule helps with bad breath because it contains powerful antibacterial properties that can target and eliminate the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Cilantro, also known as coriander, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to freshen breath. The herb's chlorophyll content helps to neutralize odors, while its natural oils fight off the bacteria that lead to bad breath. Additionally, cilantro has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. By taking a cilantro capsule, you can enjoy fresh breath and support your overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Cilantro contains compounds such as terpinen-4-ol and linalool that can inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for bad breath, thus reducing the amount of odor-causing compounds.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Cilantro has anti-inflammatory compounds such as cineole and camphor, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, reducing the potential for bad breath caused by inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Cilantro has high levels of antioxidants, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, which can help protect the mouth and gums from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the potential for bad breath caused by tissue damage.
Detoxification properties: Cilantro has been shown to have detoxifying effects, which can help remove heavy metals and toxins from the body that can contribute to bad breath.
Saliva stimulation: Cilantro can help stimulate the production of saliva, which can help wash away food particles and bacteria that can cause bad breath.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the cilantro plant are rich in chlorophyll, which helps to neutralize bad breath causing compounds.
Seeds: The seeds, also known as coriander, have antimicrobial properties that can help to reduce the bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath.
Stems: The stems of the cilantro plant contain essential oils that have been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making them effective in reducing bad breath.

Flowers: The flowers of the cilantro plant contain flavonoids that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce bad breath by reducing inflammation in the mouth and throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 cups of fresh cilantro leaves and stems, then rinse and dry them thoroughly.
Finely chop the cilantro and combine it with 1 cup of coconut oil in a saucepan; heat on low for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the cilantro-oil mixture through cheesecloth into a bowl, then let it cool before refrigerating for 2 hours to solidify.
Scoop out the solidified cilantro-oil and process it in a food processor with 1 cup of gelatin capsule powder until smooth.
Fill size 00 gelatin capsules with the cilantro-oil-gelatin mixture, using a capsule filling machine, and store the completed capsules in a cool, dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsule helps with bad breath because it contains antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that fight against the bacteria in the mouth responsible for bad breath. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help reduce inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, rosemary has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which can further help to eliminate the bacteria that cause bad breath. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Rosemary contains compounds such as camphor, cineole, and eucalyptol that have antimicrobial properties, helping to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of rosemary, due to compounds like rosmarinic acid, can reduce inflammation in the mouth and help prevent gum diseases that can cause bad breath.
Antioxidant: Rosemary is rich in antioxidants, including rosmarinic acid and carnosol, which can neutralize free radicals and protect the mouth from oxidative stress, contributing to fresher breath.
Circulation-boosting: Rosemary has been shown to improve circulation, which can help increase saliva production and flush out bacteria and food particles that can cause bad breath.

Digestion-aiding: Rosemary can help improve digestion and reduce stomach issues like bloating and gas, which can contribute to bad breath. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as cineole, camphene, and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant are rich in chlorophyll and have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing bad breath caused by oral bacteria.
Flowers: The flowers of the rosemary plant contain flavonoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to freshen breath and promote oral health.
Stems: The stems of the rosemary plant contain essential oils that have antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate the bacteria that cause bad breath.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried rosemary leaves and grind them into a fine powder, typically taking 5-10 minutes.
Mix the ground rosemary with 1 cup of unflavored gelatin or vegetarian capsule alternative, ensuring equal distribution.
Add 1 cup of water to the mixture and stir until a dough-like consistency is reached, usually requiring 3-5 minutes.
Form the dough into small capsule-sized portions, approximately 0.5-1 gram each, which may take around 10-15 minutes.
Allow the capsules to dry for 24-48 hours before storing in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint capsule helps with bad breath because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-bacterial properties that help to eliminate the bacteria and toxins that cause bad breath. Spearmint has been used for centuries as a natural remedy for oral hygiene due to its refreshing and soothing properties. The capsules release spearmint oil, which contains compounds such as carvone and limonene, that help to neutralize odors and promote fresh breath. Additionally, spearmint has been shown to have a positive effect on digestion, which can also contribute to bad breath. By taking spearmint capsules regularly, you can help to maintain fresh breath and improve your overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Spearmint capsules contain antimicrobial agents such as carvone and limonene, which help to reduce the growth of bacteria and microorganisms in the mouth that cause bad breath.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Spearmint capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, which can help alleviate bad breath caused by gum disease or inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Spearmint capsules contain antioxidants such as rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the mouth, preventing bad breath.
Fresh breath: Spearmint capsules contain menthol, which has a cooling and refreshing effect on the breath, providing immediate relief from bad breath.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the spearmint plant are rich in menthol, which helps to kill bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Flowers: The flowers of the spearmint plant contain essential oils that have antiseptic properties, making them effective in fighting bad breath.

remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried spearmint leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 0.5 to 1 teaspoon of spearmint powder and place it into size 00 empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Repeat step 2 until you have filled the desired number of capsules, ensuring each one contains an equal amount of spearmint powder.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature, away from direct sunlight, for up to 6 months.
Take 1-2 capsules daily as needed, typically 30 minutes before meals, with a full glass of water.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat bad breath with herbal capsules is by selecting those that contain natural ingredients known for their antibacterial and freshening properties. Look for supplements with ingredients such as parsley, mint, fennel, coriander, and clove. These herbs can help reduce the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Always follow the recommended dosage and consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen. Additionally, maintain good oral hygiene practices, such as brushing and flossing regularly, to ensure optimal oral health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to bad breath that are treated with herbal capsules are gum diseases and digestive issues. For instance, Chlorophyll herbal capsules are known to freshen breath and help with digestion. Additionally, herbal capsules containing extracts from plants like cloves, turmeric, and tea tree have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can improve gum health and reduce bad breath caused by oral infections. Furthermore, some herbal capsules containing probiotics can help balance the bacteria in the gut, which can also improve bad breath resulting from digestive problems. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new supplement regimen.
